2606.25533.title: Security and Privacy in Retrieval-Augmented Generation: Architectures, Threats, Defenses, and Future Directions for Building Trustworthy Systems
2606.25533.paper_summary: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) has emerged as a dominant paradigm for enhancing large language models with external knowledge. By coupling retrieval mechanisms with generative models, RAG systems improve factual grounding and adaptability across domains. However, integrating retrieval pipelines introduces new security and privacy risks that extend beyond conventional language modeling threats. Sensitive information may be exposed through retrieval indices, query logs, context construction, or federated updates, while adversarial manipulation of knowledge bases can undermine trust in generated outputs. This survey provides a comprehensive examination of privacy and security challenges across RAG systems deployed in centralized, on-device (Micro-RAG), federated, and hybrid paradigms. We present a unified taxonomy of threat surfaces spanning the retrieval, context construction, and generation stages and systematically analyze attack classes, including membership inference, index inference, poisoning, gradient leakage, and collusion. We further review architectural, algorithmic, and cryptographic defenses, highlighting privacy-utility trade-offs and deployment considerations. Finally, we outline open research challenges toward building trustworthy, secure, and resilient RAG systems for real-world applications.
2606.25533.paper_summary_zh: 檢索增強生成（RAG）已成為增強大型語言模型與外部知識的主導範式。<lb/>通過將檢索機制與生成模型結合，RAG 系統改善了事實基礎和跨領域的適應性。<lb/>然而，整合檢索管道引入了新的安全和隱私風險，這些風險超出了傳統語言建模的威脅。<lb/>敏感信息可能通過檢索索引、查詢日誌、上下文構建或聯邦更新而暴露，而對知識庫的對抗性操縱可能會削弱對生成輸出的信任。<lb/>本調查提供了對部署在集中式、設備端（Micro-RAG）、聯邦和混合範式的 RAG 系統的隱私和安全挑戰的全面檢查。<lb/>我們提出了一個統一的威脅面分類法，涵蓋檢索、上下文構建和生成階段，並系統地分析攻擊類別，包括成員推斷、索引推斷、毒化、梯度洩漏和共謀。<lb/>我們進一步回顧了架構、算法和密碼學防禦，強調了隱私-效用權衡和部署考量。<lb/>最後，我們概述了在建立可信、安全和韌性的 RAG 系統以應用於現實世界的過程中面臨的開放研究挑戰。
2606.25533.author: Balamurugan Palanisamy et.al.
2606.25533.authors: Balamurugan Palanisamy, G S S Chalapathi, Vikas Hassija, Rajkumar Buyya
